metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC1 proving command to use valid shell arithmetic: replace `test -$(wc -l < src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py) -lt -800` with `[ $(wc -l < src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py) -lt 800 ]`.
findings.finding: The AC1 proving command uses a broken double-negative shell arithmetic form (`test -$(wc -l < ...) -lt -800`) that will not work as intended: `-$(wc -l ...)` produces a flag-like token (e.g. `-743`) and `-800` is a negative integer, so `test` either errors or gives a nonsensical comparison — it does NOT reliably prove the file is under 800 lines.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the proving command with:
`lines=$(wc -l < src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py); echo "${lines} lines"; [ "${lines}" -lt 800 ]`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `_ReplayCtx` to the AC2 grep pattern so all four required symbols are verified: `grep -cE 'def replay_events|def scan_for_latest_snapshot|_ReplayCtx|_EVENT_HANDLERS' src/rebar/reducer/_replay.py` and assert the result equals 4.
findings.finding: The AC2 grep pattern checks for `replay_events`, `scan_for_latest_snapshot`, and `_EVENT_HANDLERS` but omits `_ReplayCtx`, which the same AC sentence lists as a required symbol in `_replay.py`. The proving command therefore does not verify the full set of required symbols.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 (`_replay.py` exists with required symbols)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `_ReplayCtx` to the AC #2 grep proof (e.g. `grep -c "def replay_events\|def scan_for_latest_snapshot\|_ReplayCtx\|_EVENT_HANDLERS"`) so all four named symbols are actually verified.
findings.finding: AC #2's verification grep does not include `_ReplayCtx`, even though the criterion's own text lists it as one of the four symbols that must be present in _replay.py. The proof command `grep -c "def replay_events\|def scan_for_latest_snapshot\|_EVENT_HANDLERS"` will pass even if _ReplayCtx is accidentally omitted from the new module.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC #2 proof to: `grep -c "def replay_events\|def scan_for_latest_snapshot\|_ReplayCtx\|_EVENT_HANDLERS" src/rebar/reducer/_replay.py` and assert result == 4.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a lightweight AC (e.g. `grep -v '_processors' src/rebar/reducer/__init__.py` or a docs-grep) verifying the __init__ docstring no longer references `_processors` for the moved symbols, or explicitly demote the docstring update to a non-AC note if it is intentionally unverified.
findings.finding: The `__init__` module docstring update is described as a deliverable in the What section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion to verify it was done.
findings.location: What section (importer updates paragraph) / Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0035-rc2b-snapshot-horizon-safe-replay.md` (line 9: `reducer/_processors.replay_events`) and `docs/event-schema.md` (line 42 `reducer/_processors.py` dispatch note; line 91 `_processors.replay`) to the plan's 'Importers' section as documentation files requiring retargeting to `_replay`.
findings.finding: Documentation files outside the reducer/ directory still reference the old `reducer/_processors.replay_events` / `_processors.replay` location, but the plan does not enumerate them as update targets under its 'Importers' section.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — 'Importers' bullet; docs/adr/0035-rc2b-snapshot-horizon-safe-replay.md line 9; docs/event-schema.md lines 42 and 91
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/adr/0035-rc2b-snapshot-horizon-safe-replay.md line 9, replace `reducer/_processors.replay_events` with `reducer/_replay.replay_events`. In docs/event-schema.md line 42, replace `reducer/_processors.py` with `reducer/_replay.py` for the dispatch note. In docs/event-schema.md line 91, replace `_processors.replay` with `_replay.replay_events`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out note (e.g. 'revert the single commit; _processors.py is git-recoverable in one step') or an expand-contract step (temporarily re-export the moved symbols from _processors.py with a deprecation warning) so the rollback path is documented.
findings.finding: The plan removes `replay_events`, `scan_for_latest_snapshot`, `_ReplayCtx`, and `_EVENT_HANDLERS` from `_processors.py` — a destructive step for any direct importer of those private symbols — but provides no rollback or back-out path if a missed importer is discovered post-merge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line 'Rollback' sub-section: 'This is a single atomic commit; `git revert <sha>` fully restores _processors.py and removes _replay.py with no data loss.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a grep/search step (e.g. `grep -r 'from.*_processors import\|_processors\.' tests/ src/` excluding `_processors.py` itself) to confirm no import site other than `_api.py`, `__init__.py`, and `test_reducer_tag_delta.py` references the symbols being relocated, and add the broader test suite (`pytest tests/unit/`) to AC5's proof.
findings.finding: The plan removes `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` and `TAG_DELTA` (and engine-related symbols) from `_processors.py`, names only ONE test file that must be retargeted (`test_reducer_tag_delta.py`), but does not confirm that no other test files or internal modules import these symbols directly from `._processors`. If any exist, they will silently break at import time without being covered by the AC checks.
findings.location: ## What — '_processors.py keeps ONLY the per-event process_* fold functions; drop the now-unused imports' / ## Acceptance Criteria — AC4 and AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ## What or ## Acceptance Criteria section: 'Confirm no other file imports the relocated symbols from `_processors` by running `grep -rn "_processors" tests/ src/rebar/reducer/ | grep -v "_processors.py"` and verifying the only hits are the two importer files and the one retargeted test.' Extend AC5 proof to include `pytest tests/ -q` or at minimum `pytest tests/unit/ tests/scripts/reducer/ -q`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
